name: Heroes of the Pacific
post_apply_message:
Heroes of the Pacific (2005, non-Steam) — 1920x1200 widescreen fix + auto-shortcut. The game ignores VideoQuery.exe's resolution and forces 800x600; the AlphaYellow ASI patches that.
Apply installs: (1) HeroesOfThePacificWidescreenFix.asi + its ini (Width=1920 Height=1200 FOVFactor=1.0); (2) ThirteenAG's Ultimate ASI Loader dropped in as winmm.dll (this game hooks via winmm); (3) the Steam shortcut for Heroes.exe (gospel appid 4105667313 — your existing prefix) with WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winmm=n,b" so Wine loads the native winmm loader, on GE-Proton 10.34.
Stage GE first with 'gfm stage-runner'. Then just launch — it should come up at 1920x1200 instead of 800x600. If it's still 800x600, the winmm loader didn't hook: confirm the launch option is on the shortcut, and that Heroes.exe (not HOTP.exe) is the exe that renders.
notes: Built 2026-07-10. Retail/abandonware (readmes are the original multi-language ones, not a Chemical crack). The game forces 800x600 regardless of VideoQuery.exe -> AlphaYellow Widescreen & FOV Fix (github alphayellow1/AlphaYellowWidescreenFixes, release tag heroesofthepacific, v1.1 rar) committed here (payload/widescreen, .asi 1.3MB + ini set to 1920x1200) since the download is a rar. Loader = ThirteenAG Ultimate ASI Loader v9.7.2 (same pinned zip as barnyard, single universal dinput8.dll) fetched + dropped in as winmm.dll via copy_files 'as' (new option). WINEDLLOVERRIDES winmm=n,b loads it. Exe = Heroes.exe (6.7MB; per Tony, vs HOTP.exe 3.8MB / VideoQuery.exe config). Prefix already exists at appid 4105667313 = the gospel (no drift). Save/config live in the game folder (SD-resident).
